user_story: As an anonymous user, I want to create an account with email + password so that I can access the subscriber console and start onboarding.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/signup/ accepts {email, password, display_name (optional, defaults to 'Friend')}. If PREVIEW_ACCESS_KEY is set in settings, an invite_code field must also be supplied and must match exactly, otherwise returns 403. If the email is already registered returns 409. On success, creates the User via create_user (sets username=email) and returns {access, refresh} JWT pair with HTTP 201. The refresh token is minted via RefreshToken.for_user (no pw_iat claim — the user has never rotated their password, so pw_iat=0 is acceptable at this stage). No email verification step exists.
inventory_notes: No email verification. PREVIEW_ACCESS_KEY gate is a dev-only invite wall controlled by an env var. display_name defaults to 'Friend' if omitted.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log in with my email and password so that I receive a JWT access + refresh token pair to authenticate API requests.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/login/ is backed by SimpleJWT's TokenObtainPairView with a custom serializer (EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer). The serializer uses email as the username_field, calls Django's authenticate() with email+password, rejects inactive accounts (401 no_active_account). On success it mints a token pair via get_token() which injects a pw_iat claim (unix timestamp of user.password_last_changed_at, or 0 for legacy users). Access token TTL is 15 minutes; refresh token TTL is 7 days. Both tokens carry pw_iat for force-logout enforcement.
inventory_notes: ROTATE_REFRESH_TOKENS is intentionally disabled — refresh-token blacklisting is not active. The pw_iat claim is the only force-logout mechanism (no simplejwt blacklist).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to exchange a valid refresh token for a new access token so that I can maintain my session without re-entering credentials.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/refresh/ is the standard SimpleJWT TokenRefreshView. Accepts {refresh} and returns a new {access} token. Refresh token TTL is 7 days from issuance. ROTATE_REFRESH_TOKENS is off so the refresh token itself is not rotated. If the refresh token is invalid or blacklisted, returns 401. Note: the force-logout pw_iat check in JWTAuthenticationWithRLS fires on every API request using the access token, not at refresh time — so a refresh with an old refresh token may succeed but subsequent API calls will be rejected.
inventory_notes: ROTATE_REFRESH_TOKENS and BLACKLIST_AFTER_ROTATION are both off. pw_iat enforcement happens at authenticate() time, not here.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log out so that my refresh token is invalidated and cannot be reused.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/logout/ requires IsAuthenticated (Bearer access token). Body must include {refresh}; omitting it returns 400. Calls RefreshToken(refresh_token).blacklist() via simplejwt's token_blacklist. If the token is invalid or already blacklisted, returns 400 with 'Invalid refresh token.' On success returns 204 No Content. Access tokens are short-lived (15 min) and cannot be invalidated at logout — clients must discard them.
inventory_notes: Access tokens remain valid up to 15 minutes after logout. Clients must delete their local copy.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to retrieve my current user and tenant details so that the dashboard can render my profile and subscription state.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/auth/me/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns UserSerializer data merged with TenantSerializer nested under 'tenant' key. If the user has no tenant, tenant is null. Response is cached per-tenant for 60 seconds via @tenant_cache(ttl=60, tag='tenant'). Tenant fields include status, model_tier, subscription state, trial info, container details, config versions, budget usage, and computed fields (effective_model, free_model_offer, platform_budget_exceeded, gravity_available). Returns 200.
inventory_notes: Also exists at GET /api/v1/tenants/me/ via TenantViewSet with same caching. The two endpoints overlap; auth/me/ returns user+tenant; tenants/me/ returns only TenantSerializer.

user_story: As the platform, I want every authenticated API request to validate JWT pw_iat against the user's current password_last_changed_at and set Postgres RLS session variables so that password rotations immediately invalidate all outstanding sessions.
expected_behaviour: JWTAuthenticationWithRLS (set as DEFAULT_AUTHENTICATION_CLASSES[1]) extends SimpleJWT's JWTAuthentication. get_user() fetches User with select_related('tenant') in one query. authenticate() then checks pw_iat claim: if user.password_last_changed_at is not None and token.pw_iat < password_last_changed_at.timestamp(), raises AuthenticationFailed with code 'password_rotated' (HTTP 401). On successful auth sets Postgres session vars app.tenant_id and app.user_id via set_rls_context(). Legacy tokens (pw_iat=0) are only rejected if the user has a non-null password_last_changed_at.
inventory_notes: select_related('tenant') in get_user() saves one cross-region round trip per request. RLS vars are cleared in TenantContextMiddleware.process_response() via reset_rls_context().

user_story: As an anonymous user who has forgotten my password, I want to request a reset email so that I can regain access to my account.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/password-reset/request/ is AllowAny. Accepts {email}. Applies dual rate-limiting: per-email (3 req/hr) and per-IP (5 req/hr) using Django cache; cache unavailability fails open. Regardless of whether the user exists, always returns 200 with a constant message to prevent account enumeration. If user exists and is_active, generates a uid (base64-encoded PK) and Django's default_token_generator token, builds a reset URL pointing to FRONTEND_URL/reset-password?uid=&token=, and sends a multipart HTML+text email via Mailgun. Email failures are swallowed (logged only). Returns 429 if rate limit exceeded.
dependencies: Mailgun (via Django email backend), Redis (rate-limit cache)
inventory_notes: Rate-limit counters are per IP and per email address, checked independently. Cache failure fails open to avoid locking users out. Email send failure is silently swallowed on purpose (no enumeration leak).

user_story: As an anonymous user with a reset link, I want to set a new password so that I can sign in immediately after resetting.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/password-reset/confirm/ is AllowAny. Accepts {uid, token, new_password}. Missing any field returns 400. Decodes uid from base64 to locate User; invalid uid/user returns 400 'Reset link is invalid or has expired.' Validates Django HMAC token via default_token_generator.check_token(); tokens auto-expire after PASSWORD_RESET_TIMEOUT (Django default 3 days) or when password changes. Runs Django password validators (validate_password) — failures return 400. On success: sets new password via user.set_password() (which bumps password_last_changed_at), saves with update_fields=['password','password_last_changed_at'], then mints a fresh JWT pair using EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer.get_token() (carries pw_iat). Returns {access, refresh} with 200.
inventory_notes: Must use EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer.get_token() not RefreshToken.for_user() — the latter omits pw_iat and JWTAuthenticationWithRLS would reject the fresh token for users who have a non-null password_last_changed_at.

user_story: As a subscriber or an external app (e.g. YardTalk), I want to authenticate API requests using a long-lived PAT so that I can access the API without session-based JWT tokens.
expected_behaviour: PersonalAccessTokenAuthentication (DEFAULT_AUTHENTICATION_CLASSES[0], checked first) intercepts Authorization headers starting with 'Bearer pat_'. SHA-256 hashes the raw token for DB lookup. Fetches PersonalAccessToken with select_related('user','user__tenant'). Returns 401 if not found. Checks is_valid: revoked_at not null → 401 'Token has been revoked.'; expires_at exceeded → 401 'Token has expired.' On valid token, sets RLS context, stamps last_used_at (throttled to once per minute via update vs save), and stashes the PAT on request.auth_pat for downstream scope checks. Returns (user, pat).
inventory_notes: PAT auth runs before JWT auth in the auth chain. last_used_at update is rate-throttled to once/minute per token to avoid a cross-region write on every request.

user_story: As the platform, I want PAT-authenticated requests to be limited to their declared scopes so that a token issued for session ingestion cannot access unrelated endpoints.
expected_behaviour: HasPATScope (and subclasses HasSessionsWriteScope, HasSessionsReadScope) checks request.auth_pat. If auth_pat is None (JWT-authed request), the scope check passes through — JWT sessions have full access. If auth_pat is set, the required_scope must be in pat.scopes. ALLOWED_PAT_SCOPES currently contains only 'sessions:write' and 'sessions:read'. Views using these permission classes return 403 if the PAT lacks the required scope.
inventory_notes: JWT-authenticated requests bypass scope checks entirely. Only two scopes exist. Scopes are stored as a JSON list on the PersonalAccessToken row.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to create a named personal access token so that external apps can authenticate on my behalf.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/tokens/create/ requires IsAuthenticated (JWT). Rate-limited by UserPATMintHourThrottle (10/hour per user). Accepts {name, scopes (optional, defaults to ['sessions:write','sessions:read']), expires_in_days (optional, 1-365)}. Validates scopes against ALLOWED_PAT_SCOPES — invalid scopes return 400. Generates a cryptographically random token (secrets.token_urlsafe(32) prefixed with 'pat_'), stores only the SHA-256 hash and first-8-char prefix. Returns {id, name, token (raw, shown ONCE), token_prefix, scopes, expires_at, created_at, warning} with 201. Raw token is never stored.
inventory_notes: Raw token is shown exactly once in the response. Token format: 'pat_' + base64url(32 random bytes). Hash stored is SHA-256 of the full 'pat_...' string.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list my active personal access tokens so that I can see what apps have access and when they were last used.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/auth/tokens/ requires IsAuthenticated (JWT). Returns all non-revoked (revoked_at__isnull=True) PersonalAccessTokens for request.user. Serialized fields: id, name, token_prefix, scopes, last_used_at, expires_at, revoked_at, created_at. Does not return the raw token (stored only at creation). Returns 200 with a list.
inventory_notes: Filter is revoked_at__isnull=True so expired-but-not-revoked tokens still appear.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to revoke a personal access token so that an app can no longer authenticate using it.
expected_behaviour: DELETE /api/v1/auth/tokens/<uuid:token_id>/ requires IsAuthenticated (JWT). Looks up PersonalAccessToken by id and user (ownership check) — not found returns 404. If already revoked (revoked_at is not None) returns 400 'Token already revoked.' Otherwise stamps revoked_at=now() via save(update_fields=['revoked_at']). Returns 204 No Content. After revocation, PersonalAccessTokenAuthentication rejects any request bearing this token with 'Token has been revoked.'
inventory_notes: Ownership enforced by user=request.user filter. No admin override path on this endpoint.

user_story: As a subscriber completing sign-up on the web, I want the SPA to mint a short-lived authorization code so that the iOS app can exchange it for a JWT pair without re-entering credentials.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/authorize/ requires IsAuthenticated (JWT). Rate-limited by AuthorizeMintMinuteThrottle (30/minute per user). Accepts {code_challenge (S256 base64url), code_challenge_method ('S256'), redirect_uri}. Validates: method must be 'S256'; code_challenge must be non-empty and match base64url alphabet (_B64URL_RE); redirect_uri must be in settings.AUTH_ALLOWED_REDIRECT_URIS (default ['nbhd://auth/callback']). On failure returns 400 {error: 'invalid_request'}. On success generates a 32-byte cryptographically random code, stores only the SHA-256 hash plus the code_challenge in OAuthAuthorizationCode row with TTL from AUTH_EXCHANGE_CODE_TTL_SECONDS (default 300s). Returns {code: <raw_code>} with 200.
inventory_notes: Only S256 method supported — plain not allowed. redirect_uri whitelist is in AUTH_ALLOWED_REDIRECT_URIS env var. The raw code travels only in the JSON response body; the hash is stored.

user_story: As the iOS app, I want to exchange a PKCE authorization code and code_verifier for a JWT pair so that the user is signed into the app without entering a password.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/exchange/ is AllowAny. Rate-limited by ExchangeMinuteThrottle (30/minute per client IP via X-Forwarded-For). Accepts {code, code_verifier, redirect_uri}. Missing fields → 400 {error: invalid_grant}. Looks up OAuthAuthorizationCode by SHA-256(code); not found → 400 invalid_grant (logged). Inside a SELECT FOR UPDATE atomic transaction: checks consumed_at is None and now() < expires_at; redirect_uri matched via hmac.compare_digest (constant-time); PKCE verified by re-deriving S256(verifier) and constant-time comparing to stored code_challenge. User must be active. Code is marked consumed_at=now() inside the lock. Returns {access, refresh} minted via EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer.get_token() (carries pw_iat) with 200. ALL failures return identical 400 {error: invalid_grant}.
inventory_notes: select_for_update prevents double-spend race. All failure reasons logged server-side only, never surfaced to caller. pw_iat claim required (not RefreshToken.for_user).

user_story: As the platform, I want every password change to automatically bump password_last_changed_at so that all outstanding JWTs are invalidated without needing a token blacklist.
expected_behaviour: User.set_password() overrides AbstractUser.set_password() to call super() then set self.password_last_changed_at = timezone.now(). Similarly User.set_unusable_password() calls super() and sets the same stamp. This means the stamp is bumped on: admin password changes, password reset confirm, createsuperuser, and the rotate_all_passwords management command. The change in memory is committed only when the caller explicitly saves — callers must include 'password_last_changed_at' in update_fields to avoid silently dropping the bump.
inventory_notes: The PasswordResetConfirmView and rotate_all_passwords explicitly include 'password_last_changed_at' in update_fields. Legacy users (pw_iat=0 tokens) are only rejected if password_last_changed_at is non-null.

user_story: As the platform, I want every request to set and clear Postgres session-level RLS variables so that row-level security policies are enforced for the duration of each request.
expected_behaviour: TenantContextMiddleware.process_request() sets thread-local _tenant_context.tenant and calls set_rls_context(tenant_id, user_id) if user is authenticated and has a tenant. set_rls_context() issues a single SELECT SET_CONFIG(...) statement bundling all variables to minimize cross-region round trips. process_response() clears the context and calls reset_rls_context(), which issues another SET_CONFIG reset only when _tenant_context.rls_dirty=True (skipping unauthenticated/404 responses to avoid unnecessary DB connections). UserTimezoneMiddleware activates the user's IANA timezone for the request lifecycle.
inventory_notes: rls_dirty flag optimization avoids opening a DB connection on every response for unauthenticated requests. JWTAuthenticationWithRLS also calls set_rls_context after auth — this may set vars twice on authenticated requests, but is idempotent.

user_story: As an admin/operator, I want to force-rotate all users' passwords and email them reset links so that a security event results in every session being invalidated fleet-wide.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py rotate_all_passwords --reason=<string> [--dry-run] [--since=<ISO datetime>]. Excludes the PLATFORM_OWNER_EMAIL user. Iterates all users skipping: those with no email, those whose password_last_changed_at >= --since (idempotency). For each eligible user: calls user.set_unusable_password() (bumps password_last_changed_at, invalidates all JWTs), saves with update_fields, generates a uid+token, sends a reset email using privacy_rotation_2026/email_1_* templates. Email send failures are logged but do not stop iteration. Prints rotated/skipped/failed counts. Idempotent: re-running with the same --since skips already-rotated users.
inventory_notes: Also callable via QStash as rotate_all_passwords_task() with hardcoded 'june-2026-privacy-hygiene' reason. PLATFORM_OWNER_EMAIL must be set or operator locks themselves out.
